news.title: World leaders want American AI. They just don’t want America to be able to turn it off.
news.description: [TECHCRUNCH] French President Macron and Indian PM Modi raised alarms at the G7 summit that the U.S. could cut off access to American AI overnight — a fear the Anthropic blackout just made real.

news.title: Anthropic becomes first AI startup to join the Frontier carbon removal coalition
news.description: [TECHCRUNCH] Anthropic has joined the Frontier coalition, which received another $915M in pledges to fund carbon removal projects.

news.title: Social media’s next evolution: user-controlled algorithms
news.description: [TECHCRUNCH] Social media feeds are becoming more customizable as platforms like Threads, Instagram, and TikTok introduce tools that let users directly influence the algorithms powering their recommendations.

news.title: World model maker Odyssey nabs $1.45B valuation backed by Amazon and other big names
news.description: [TECHCRUNCH] World models are the next big thing in AI beyond LLMs and, with this round, Odyssey has cemented itself as one of the startups to watch.

news.title: Only 16 percent of Americans think AI will have a positive impact on society, a new study shows
news.description: [TECHCRUNCH] Although Wall Street loves AI, every day Americans are significantly less optimistic about the industry, a new report from Pew Research shows.

news.title: Google bets on Gemini to reinvent the smart home speaker
news.description: [TECHCRUNCH] Google is betting generative AI can breathe new life into the smart speaker. The company's new $99.99 Google Home Speaker replaces the rigid commands of the Google Assistant era with more conversational Gemini interactions.

news.description: [U.S. NEWS & WORLD REPORT] Top artificial intelligence executives are gathering Wednesday in France against a backdrop of growing calls for tech sovereignty in Europe, fueled by concerns about American dominance in the industry.
